title: Philips Healthcare — Fixed Fee Service Delivery Proposal
description: Full PS proposal with executive summary, approach, timeline, scope summaries, assumptions, governance, RACI, pricing, risks, next steps

slides.title: Executive Summary
slides.overview:
Philips Healthcare operates across a highly complex data landscape — SAP, Salesforce, unstructured clinical content, and a sprawling multi-cloud environment spanning Azure, AWS, GCP, Databricks, Teradata, and legacy SQL Server and Oracle systems. Without a deliberate data foundation, AI and analytics initiatives risk being built on unstable ground: inconsistent data, siloed pipelines, brittle integrations, and no clear path to production readiness.

The goal of this engagement is to establish that foundation and repeatable framework — and ensure Philips' internal teams are equipped to sustain and extend it independently.
outcomes: Foundational Snowflake environment configured to best practices
RBAC, security, and Horizon governance guidance
Data ingestion enablement for your sources
Essential transformation patterns to improve data readiness and clarity
Medallion Architecture to enable Snowflake Intelligence
Project management, milestones, and weekly cadence

phases.title: Foundational Discovery & Roadmap
phases.description: Define the blueprint: align on scope, map source systems, and design the foundation that will scale beyond this engagement.
activities: Align on business priorities, success criteria, and expansion goals
Identify priority sources and map the future ingestion roadmap
Review source system architecture, data availability, and access requirements
Validate Snowflake access, identity, and cloud storage readiness
Define the scalable framework architecture and milestone execution plan
phases.outcome: A clear blueprint and expansion roadmap before build begins

phases.title: Guided Implementation & Advisory
phases.description: Build the reusable framework: implement a secure foundation and scalable patterns your team can use to onboard new sources and deploy additional use cases.
activities: Establish a secure, governance-ready Snowflake foundation (RBAC, tagging, policies)
Implement medallion architecture as a reusable template for future data domains
Enable initial source ingestion with repeatable patterns and playbooks
Build reusable transformation standards your team can apply to new sources
Productionize AI use cases with evaluation frameworks, observability, and handoff playbooks
phases.outcome: A secure, scalable foundation and reusable framework your team can extend

phases.title: Enablement, Tracking & Future Roadmapping
phases.description: Ensure delivery quality, validate the blueprint, and equip Philips to operate and expand independently.
activities: Weekly working sessions and backlog reviews
Proactive risk and dependency management
Milestone check-ins to validate framework completeness and reusability
Final review to confirm blueprint outcomes and team readiness
Roadmap workshop: define the next wave of source systems and use cases
phases.outcome: A validated blueprint and roadmap — ready for Philips to operate, expand, and scale

slides.title: High Level Timeline
slides.subtitle: Based on the scope, we recommend the following timeline for the delivery of the engagement.

slides.title: Security — Assessment and Overhaul
slides.outcome: Your Snowflake account is established and configured with fundamental authentication, role based access control (RBAC), and data protection for any sensitive data or PII (data masking, row level access, aggregation policies).

milestones.name: Security Assessment
activities: Review current authentication methods
Audit existing RBAC structure
Identify sensitive/PII columns

milestones.name: RBAC Overhaul
activities: Implement functional role hierarchy
Define object-level grant matrix
Apply network policies

milestones.name: Data Protection
activities: Apply dynamic data masking policies
Configure row access policies
Enable audit logging

slides.title: Medallion Architecture — Design for Scale
slides.outcome: A modern data architecture positioning Philips Healthcare to accelerate data readiness and use case deployment.

milestones.name: Architecture Design
activities: Define Bronze/Silver/Gold layer standards
Establish naming conventions and schema patterns
Design dynamic tables and transformation workflows

milestones.name: Initial Implementation
activities: Implement Bronze ingestion layer for priority sources
Build Silver transformation templates
Activate Gold analytical views

milestones.name: Governance Layer
activities: Apply tags and classification policies
Enable Data Metric Functions for quality monitoring
Document lineage and ownership

slides.title: Snowflake's Role & Delivery Assumptions
left_items: Snowflake's advisory work is performed in development and validation environments only
Required security approvals, cloud service provider resource access, and networking rules are in place before engagement start
Engagement does not include re-factoring or modification of existing custom code developed by the customer
Engagement does not include production execution, operations, or ongoing monitoring
Snowflake provides guidance and validation; customers approve and execute final operationalization steps
right_items: Engagement focuses on a single, clearly defined business priority and supporting data flow
Source data must be reasonably structured and ready for onboarding at engagement start
Data onboarding leverages Snowflake-native features and recommended patterns
Data consumption layer activation includes connectivity, access, and validation for analytics — not application or dashboard development
Any expansion of scope requires a change order

slides.title: Customer Responsibilities & Collaboration Assumptions
slides.intro: SD Engagement is a collaborative model — Snowflake provides advisory guidance and enablement, but does not replace customer engineers or operational teams.

items: Assign a dedicated technical contact and project coordinator
Ensure SME availability for weekly working sessions
Maintain availability for approvals and decisions within 48 hours
Provide access to source systems and development environments
Ensure source data is available and reasonably structured
Provision Snowflake account access for Snowflake engineers prior to kickoff
Approve architecture and security design documents before build begins
Provide timely sign-off on milestone deliverables
Manage internal change management and stakeholder alignment

rows.purpose: Review status, risks, blockers, upcoming activities
Review completed items, prioritize upcoming sprint

slides.title: RACI — Who Is Doing What & When?

legend.R: Responsible — The group who does the work.
legend.A: Accountable — The group ultimately answerable for task completion.
legend.C: Consulted — The group whose opinion is sought before a decision.
legend.I: Inform — Group who need to be kept up-to-date on progress.

slides.title: Engagement Estimates and Fees
slides.note: Fixed-price, fixed-outcome engagement. This proposal is based on a clearly defined scope of work, guaranteeing the delivery of the specified outcomes for a fixed price.

rows.responsibilities: Project management, stakeholder alignment, risk management, weekly status reporting
Architecture design, security, medallion implementation, AI productionization
Data ingestion, transformation, documentation, knowledge transfer
AI use case productionization, model evaluation, Cortex Intelligence setup

slides.title: Typical Areas of Risk & How We Mitigate

rows.mitigation: Confirm all access requirements in kickoff; document prerequisites before engagement start.
Fixed-fee scope with formal change order process for any additions.
Dedicated customer PM and SME availability confirmed as contract prerequisite.
Discovery phase identifies risks before build begins; architecture sign-off gates build.

slides.title: Next Steps — Ensuring a Smooth and Successful Start

steps.label: Align on Scope
steps.description: Guarantee a single, clear agreement on what will be delivered.

steps.label: Scope Using Best Practices
steps.description: Ensure the project is scoped using Snowflake's proven best practices for successful delivery.

steps.label: Secure Day 1 Access
steps.description: Prevent delays by ensuring our team can start securely and efficiently on day one.

steps.label: Assign Best-Prepared Experts
steps.description: Ensure you have the best-prepared experts and set the entire project up for effective execution.

steps.label: Align Everyone on the Plan
steps.description: Align everyone on goals, roles, and the plan, setting the project up for fast, effective execution.
